findings.title: Stage-2 latent restoration network R_theta (SFHformer) is absent; Stage-2 only reconstructs through the VAE
findings.claim: Stage-2 forward calls net_g (a RAVAEHFLora that wraps only the VAE encoder/decoder, see LHVAE_hflora_arch.py:122-133 where forward does out = self.vae(x)); the recon it returns is an autoencoder reconstruction of the degraded input lq, never the output of a latent restoration network.
findings.concern: The paper's Stage 2 (Eq. 7-9, Sec 4.2) restores via R_theta on the latent (z_res = R_theta(z_deg); Table 5c says R_theta is SFHformer), but no restoration network exists anywhere in the repo (grep for SFHformer/R_theta/net_r returns nothing), so the released code cannot produce the restored images behind any reported metric.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the latent restoration network R_theta (SFHformer/Restormer/NAFNet) and wire it into the Stage-2 forward path (Eq. 9 uses D(R_theta(E(I_deg)))); confirm whether the published numbers used R_theta and not bare VAE reconstruction.
findings.reason: VAEadapter_model.py lines 132-142 match the quote exactly, and LHVAE_hflora_arch.py lines 122-133 confirm that RAVAEHFLora.forward calls only self.vae(x) with no separate restoration network; no SFHformer/R_theta class exists in the repo.

findings.title: No evaluation, inference, or test entrypoint exists; no table/figure number is reproducible
findings.claim: The only executable entrypoint in the repo is basicsr/train.py (training). A repo-wide search for files matching *test*/*eval*/*infer*/*demo* returns nothing, and no script computes PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/FID over a benchmark, runs the α sweep, or produces the motivation figures.
findings.concern: Per the result-traceability rule, every value in Tables 1-5 and Fig. 2 must trace to code that computes it; here no script computes any reported number, so all reported results are untraceable from the artefact.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the evaluation/inference scripts (with the exact commands and the benchmark loaders) used to produce Tables 1-5, including the α-sweep and ablation drivers.
findings.reason: train.py lines 215-217 match the quote exactly, and a repo-wide search confirms no test.py, eval.py, infer*.py, or demo*.py exists anywhere in the repo.

findings.title: Empty README, no dependency spec, no pretrained weights, no datasets — repo is not runnable end-to-end
findings.claim: README.md contains only the title line (no install/run/results instructions). There is no requirements.txt / setup.py / environment.yml; no pretrained VAE or LoRA weights (only basicsr/metrics/niqe_pris_params.npz, a metric helper, ships); no datasets; and configs point pretrain_vae_path/local_weight_path/dataroot_* at non-existent placeholder paths (./weights/stage1_eqvae.pth, ./weights/dinov2_vits14.pth, ./datasets/train/gt).
findings.concern: A minimally complete submission needs a dependency spec, training + evaluation code, weights (or a fetch), and a README with reproduce commands; none is present, and the NeurIPS checklist Q5 states code is unreleased ('We will release the source code upon acceptance'), so the environment cannot be rebuilt and results cannot be reproduced.
findings.resolution: Authors: add a pinned dependency list, a README with exact reproduce commands and a results table, and either ship the trained Stage-1/Stage-2 weights and DINOv2 checkpoint or provide resolvable download links plus dataset access instructions.
findings.paper_ref: NeurIPS checklist Q5 (Open access to data and code) = [No]
findings.reason: README.md contains only '# Latent-Harmony' (one line), confirmed by direct read; no requirements.txt, setup.py, or environment.yml found anywhere in the repo.

findings.title: Stage-2 config requests arch type 'RAVAE', which is never registered → KeyError at startup
findings.quote:
cfg = deepcopy(vae_config)
arch_type = cfg.pop('type')
self.vae = ARCH_REGISTRY.get(arch_type)(**cfg)
findings.claim: RAVAEHFLora.__init__ reads vae_config['type'] and calls ARCH_REGISTRY.get(arch_type). configs/stage2_hflora.yml:42 sets vae_config.type: RAVAE, but the only arches registered via @ARCH_REGISTRY.register() in *_arch.py are RAVAE_EQ, RAVAEHFLora, LoRAConv2d, UNetDiscriminatorSN (see _audit_code/out/arch_registry.csv). No class named 'RAVAE' is registered.
findings.concern: Registry.get('RAVAE') raises KeyError (basicsr/utils/registry.py:62-66 raises when name is missing), so building the Stage-2 network with the shipped config crashes before any training/eval can run.
findings.resolution: Authors: either register the intended backbone under the name 'RAVAE' or change configs/stage2_hflora.yml vae_config.type to 'RAVAE_EQ' (the actually-registered arch), and confirm which VAE class produced the paper's Stage-2 results.
findings.reason: LHVAE_hflora_arch.py lines 36-38 match the quote exactly; grep confirms only RAVAE_EQ, RAVAEHFLora, LoRAConv2d, and UNetDiscriminatorSN are registered — no 'RAVAE' class — while configs/stage2_hflora.yml line 42 sets vae_config.type: RAVAE.

findings.title: experiments_root hardcoded to the authors' cluster path /fs-computility/... breaks any off-cluster run
findings.quote:
if is_train:
    experiments_root = osp.join('/fs-computility/ai4sData/liuyidi/model', 'experiments', opt['name'])
    opt['path']['experiments_root'] = experiments_root
    opt['path']['models'] = osp.join(experiments_root, 'models')
    opt['path']['training_states'] = osp.join(experiments_root, 'training_states')
    opt['path']['log'] = experiments_root
    opt['path']['visualization'] = osp.join(experiments_root, 'visualization')
findings.claim: On every training run, all output directories (models, logs, training_states, visualization) are placed under the absolute path /fs-computility/ai4sData/liuyidi/model/experiments/<name>, ignoring root_path / cwd; train.py then calls make_exp_dirs(opt) and copy_opt_file to that root.
findings.concern: This absolute path exists only on the authors' machine; on any other system make_exp_dirs / copyfile will fail (no such directory / permission denied), so the provided training entrypoint cannot run as shipped without editing the source.
findings.resolution: Authors: derive experiments_root from root_path (e.g. osp.join(root_path, 'experiments', opt['name'])) or expose it as a config/CLI option, as upstream BasicSR does.
findings.reason: options.py lines 158-164 match the quote exactly; the hardcoded absolute path /fs-computility/ai4sData/liuyidi/model is confirmed present in the shipped source.

findings.title: Shipped configs are labelled 'example' with placeholder data paths and generic hyper-parameters, not the configs that produced the paper numbers
findings.quote:
name: stage2_hflora_example
model_type: VAEadapter
num_gpu: 1
manual_seed: 123
findings.claim: Both configs are named *_example (stage1_eqvae_example, stage2_hflora_example), use generic single-GPU settings, batch_size_per_gpu: 2, total_iter: 100000, and placeholder data roots ./datasets/train/gt etc.; they do not encode the six-degradation / four-degradation training recipes, per-degradation datasets, or the α and LoRA-rank settings that back Tables 1-5.
findings.concern: The paper reports specific UHD all-in-one results (Tables 1-2) and an α sweep (Table 5d), but the released configs are illustrative templates, so the exact experimental settings used for the paper cannot be recovered from the repo.
findings.resolution: Authors: release the actual training/eval configs (data manifests, degradation mix, iters, LoRA rank/alpha, λ_Inv/λ_Eqv/λ_hf_fid/λ_gan, α values) used for each reported table.
findings.reason: configs/stage2_hflora.yml lines 1-4 match the quote exactly with 'stage2_hflora_example' name, num_gpu: 1, placeholder data paths confirmed; the config is clearly a template rather than a paper-reproduction config.
